k k to solve this problem we need to handle the array carefully since the rules change every third index. the first step is to compute prefix sums for both the values and their squares to allow fast calculations. when the index is a multiple of three we use the squared difference formula which requires the sum of squares. for other indices we use the standard absolute difference approach that relies on the sum of values.

***

the algorithm runs in linear time by leveraging the sorted nature of the input array to avoid nested loops. we can derive the result for any position using simple arithmetic with the precomputed prefix arrays. this method ensures we stay within the strict time limits even for large inputs. k k the final output array will contain the correct sums based on the specific rule for each index.
